title: Nestlé S.A. ESG Governance & Climate Accountability Analysis
text:
Academic Project 2024–2025 | Songting Chen | DCU Accounting & Finance


This project analyses Nestlé’s corporate governance structure, ESG accountability, climate risk management, and sustainability performance using real-world data from the 2024 Non-Financial Statement, Corporate Governance Report, and public audits.
Using Python, we visualise: - Board composition & independence - ESG-linked executive remuneration - Plastic reduction target performance - Governance accountability gaps
This analysis applies Agency Theory and Stakeholder Theory to evaluate governance effectiveness in delivering genuine climate action.






View Python Source Code
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.express as px

# Optimize global plotting style for high-end aesthetics (differentiated from Project 1)
plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-white')
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'Inter'  # Unified professional font
plt.rcParams['axes.facecolor'] = '#fafafa'  # Soft neutral background
plt.rcParams['axes.edgecolor'] = '#e0e0e0'  # Subtle border
plt.rcParams['axes.labelweight'] = 'bold'  # Bold axis labels for clarity



System Setup: Environment configured with Pandas for data processing and Matplotlib/Plotly for professional financial visualisations.







View Python Source Code
# Core governance and sustainability metrics (structured for clarity)
metrics = [
    "Board Independence (%)",
    "ESG in Annual Bonus (%)",
    "LTI Linked to Climate (%)",
    "Plastic Reduction Target Achieved (%)",
    "Plastic Reduction Goal (%)"
]

values = [92, 15, 20, 21.3, 33]

# Board structure (enhanced data for richer visualization)
board_data = {
    "Category": ["Total Directors", "Independent Directors", "Female Directors", "Male Directors"],
    "Count": [13, 12, 4, 9],
    "Color": ["#2E86AB", "#A23B72", "#F18F01", "#3C6E71"]  # Custom color mapping
}

# Executive incentive breakdown (detailed for advanced visualization)
incentive_data = {
    'Component': ['Base Salary', 'Annual Bonus (ESG Linked)', 'Long-Term Incentive (Climate)'],
    'Weight (%)': [60, 15, 20],
    'Description': ['Fixed compensation', 'Tied to ESG performance metrics', 'Linked to climate targets']
}



Data Foundation: KPIs derived from Nestlé’s 2024 Corporate Governance and Non-Financial Statements, focusing on board structure and incentive alignment.







View Python Source Code
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Suppress font warnings
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'sans-serif'
plt.rcParams['font.sans-serif'] = ['Arial', 'DejaVu Sans', 'sans-serif']

# Prepare Radar Chart data
num_vars = len(metrics)
angles = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, num_vars, endpoint=False).tolist()
values_radar = values + values[:1]  # Close the loop
angles += angles[:1]

# Reduced figsize (5x5) for a more compact radar chart
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 5), subplot_kw=dict(polar=True))

# Draw the radar plot
ax.fill(angles, values_radar, color='#2E86AB', alpha=0.25)
ax.plot(angles, values_radar, color='#2E86AB', linewidth=1.5, marker='o', markersize=4)

# Professional styling
ax.set_theta_offset(np.pi / 2)
ax.set_theta_direction(-1)
plt.xticks(angles[:-1], metrics, fontsize=8) # Smaller font for cleaner look

ax.set_rlabel_position(0)
plt.yticks([25, 50, 75, 100], ["25%", "50%", "75%", "100%"], color="grey", size=7)
plt.ylim(0, 100)

plt.title('ESG Performance Radar (2024)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', pad=20)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 1






Key Insight: Nestlé shows exceptional Board Independence (92%), providing a strong foundation for oversight.
Observation: There is a visible skew towards governance structure over environmental target achievement.







View Python Source Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df_board = pd.DataFrame(board_data)

# Reduced size for a more sophisticated web appearance
fig_tree = px.treemap(df_board, path=['Category'], values='Count',
                 title='Board Composition Hierarchy',
                 color='Count',
                 color_continuous_scale='RdBu',
                 width=600, height=350)

fig_tree.update_layout(
    title_font_size=14,
    title_font_weight='bold',
    margin=dict(t=40, l=10, r=10, b=10),
    plot_bgcolor='white',
    paper_bgcolor='white'
)

fig_tree.show()




                            
                                            


Figure 2





Key Insight: Board independence is nearly absolute (12/13 directors), significantly minimizing agency costs and potential management entrenchment.
Data Point: Gender diversity stands at 31%, highlighting a secondary focus area for future governance evolution.







View Python Source Code
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Using a much slimmer height (1.6) for a refined dashboard appearance
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 1.6))

# Suppress font warnings
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'sans-serif'
plt.rcParams['font.sans-serif'] = ['Arial', 'DejaVu Sans', 'sans-serif']

# Data points
categories = ['Plastic Reduction']
actual_val = 21.3
target_val = 33

# Plot target bar (background) and actual bar (foreground)
plt.barh(categories, [target_val], color='#E0E0E0', label='Target (33%)', height=0.4)
plt.barh(categories, [actual_val], color='#A23B72', label='Actual (21.3%)', height=0.2)

# Add target vertical line
plt.axvline(x=target_val, color='#C73E1D', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5)

# Add value annotations
plt.text(target_val + 0.5, 0, f'Target: {target_val}%', color='#C73E1D', 
         va='center', fontweight='bold', fontsize=9)
plt.text(actual_val - 0.8, 0, f'{actual_val}%', color='white', 
         va='center', ha='right', fontweight='bold', fontsize=8)

# Styling and minimalist cleanup
plt.title('Progress Toward 2025 Sustainability Goal', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', pad=10)
plt.xlabel('Reduction Percentage (%)', fontsize=9)
plt.xlim(0, 45)
plt.yticks([]) # Hide y-axis text to save space and look cleaner
plt.legend(loc='lower right', fontsize=8, frameon=False)
plt.grid(axis='x', linestyle=':', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 3






Key Insight: An 11.7% gap currently exists between the 2024 performance (21.3%) and the 2025 plastic reduction goal (33%).
Strategic Risk: Accelerated investment in circular economy infrastructure is required to mitigate potential greenwashing accusations.







View Python Source Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df_inc = pd.DataFrame(incentive_data)

# Reduced size for a professional dashboard look
fig = px.pie(df_inc, values='Weight (%)', names='Component',
             title='Executive Remuneration Structure',
             hole=0.45,  # Slightly larger hole for a more modern look
             color_discrete_map={
                 'Base Salary': '#2E86AB',
                 'Annual Bonus (ESG Linked)': '#F18F01',
                 'Long-Term Incentive (Climate)': '#A23B72'
             },
             width=550, height=380)

# Enhance aesthetics and remove Inter font dependency
fig.update_layout(
    title_font_size=13,
    title_font_weight='bold',
    margin=dict(t=50, l=10, r=10, b=10),
    plot_bgcolor='#fafafa',
    paper_bgcolor='white',
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.2, xanchor="center", x=0.5)
)

# Optimize text and hover tooltips
fig.update_traces(
    textposition='inside',
    textinfo='percent',
    hovertemplate='<b>%{label}</b><br>Weight: %{value}%<br>%{customdata}',
    customdata=df_inc['Description']
)

fig.show()




                            
                                            


Figure 4





Key Insight: 40% of executive variable pay is directly linked to ESG and Climate targets, aligning management incentives with long-term sustainability.
Theoretical Alignment: This structure utilizes Agency Theory to ensure executives prioritize environmental outcomes alongside financial returns.








Strong Governance Structure

92% independent directors (12/13) – exceeds industry average of 75%
Dedicated Sustainability Committee at board level, with quarterly ESG audits
ESG metrics integrated into executive pay (35% of variable remuneration linked to sustainability)

Critical Accountability Gap

Only 21.3% achieved against a 33% plastic reduction target (2025 goal)
Governance design (policy, structure) exceeds operational delivery (execution, outcomes)
Tension between corporate lobbying (e.g., on packaging regulations) and public sustainability commitments

Theoretical Framework

Agency Theory: ESG KPIs align management incentives with shareholder climate risk management
Stakeholder Theory: Sustainability programs address environmental, social, and regulatory stakeholder demands
Hybrid model reveals implementation gaps between policy design and on-the-ground practice






Establish a dedicated Climate Oversight Committee (independent directors only) to monitor target progress
Allocate 30% of Capital Expenditure (CapEx) to decarbonisation and circular economy initiatives
Mandate board-level climate literacy training (aligned with TCFD recommendations)
Implement quarterly public disclosures on plastic reduction progress to enhance transparency







Data Visualisation: Advanced proficiency in Python (Pandas, Matplotlib, Plotly) for financial and ESG data storytelling.
Governance Analysis: Deep understanding of Agency Theory and Board Accountability frameworks
ESG Compliance: Applied knowledge of ISSB, CSRD, and TCFD sustainability reporting standards.
Financial Auditing: Quantitative synthesis of Corporate Governance Reports and Non-Financial Statements.
Strategic Modeling: Performed Gap Analysis for climate targets and executive remuneration alignment.

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
Technical Implementation (Python)
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots


NAVY = "#003366"
GOLD = "#C5A059"
GREY = "#F2F2F2"
DARK_GREY = "#404040"


plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-white')
plt.rcParams.update({
    'font.family': 'sans-serif',
    'font.sans-serif': ['Arial'],
    'axes.edgecolor': DARK_GREY,
    'axes.labelcolor': DARK_GREY,
    'xtick.color': DARK_GREY,
    'ytick.color': DARK_GREY
})

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
Technical Implementation (Python)
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots


NAVY = "#003366"
GOLD = "#C5A059"
GREY = "#F2F2F2"
DARK_GREY = "#404040"


plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-white')
plt.rcParams.update({
    'font.family': 'sans-serif',
    'font.sans-serif': ['Arial'],
    'axes.edgecolor': DARK_GREY,
    'axes.labelcolor': DARK_GREY,
    'xtick.color': DARK_GREY,
    'ytick.color': DARK_GREY
})

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
2. Core Financial Datasets (2022–2025)


Technical Implementation (Python)
#data
financial_data = {
    'Year': [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025],
    'Revenue': [185000, 210000, 235000, 268000],
    'COGS': [95000, 108000, 119000, 132000],
    'Op_Expenses': [61500, 67800, 74500, 86200],
    'Net_Profit': [28500, 34200, 41500, 49800],
    'Current_Assets': [46000, 52000, 59000, 67000],
    'Current_Liabilities': [22000, 24000, 26000, 29000]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(financial_data)
df['Gross_Profit'] = df['Revenue'] - df['COGS']
df['GPM'] = (df['Gross_Profit'] / df['Revenue']) * 100
df['NPM'] = (df['Net_Profit'] / df['Revenue']) * 100
df['Current_Ratio'] = df['Current_Assets'] / df['Current_Liabilities']

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
3. Financial Ratio Benchmark

Technical Implementation (Python)
styled_df = df[['Year', 'GPM', 'NPM', 'Current_Ratio']].copy()
styled_df.columns = ['Fiscal Year', 'Gross Margin (%)', 'Net Margin (%)', 'Liquidity (Current Ratio)']

print(f"{'STRATEGIC KPI SUMMARY':^60}")
print("="*60)
print(styled_df.to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f'{x:.2f}'))
print("="*60)




Table 1: Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Summary



                   STRATEGIC KPI SUMMARY                    
============================================================
 Fiscal Year  Gross Margin (%)  Net Margin (%)  Liquidity (Current Ratio)
        2022             48.65           15.41                       2.09
        2023             48.57           16.29                       2.17
        2024             49.36           17.66                       2.27
        2025             50.75           18.58                       2.31
============================================================

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
4. Profitability Engine: Dual-Axis Trend Analysis


Technical Implementation (Python)
# Reduce figsize to (7, 4.5) for a more compact output
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4.5))

# Plot Revenue bars (using defined NAVY color)
bars = ax1.bar(df['Year'], df['Revenue'], color=NAVY, alpha=0.85, label='Total Revenue', width=0.4)
ax1.set_ylabel('Revenue (EUR)', fontweight='bold', color=NAVY, fontsize=9)
ax1.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor=NAVY, labelsize=8)

# Create secondary y-axis for profit margin
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(df['Year'], df['NPM'], color=GOLD, marker='D', linewidth=2.5, markersize=6, label='Net Profit Margin (%)')
ax2.set_ylabel('Net Margin (%)', fontweight='bold', color=GOLD, fontsize=9)
ax2.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor=GOLD, labelsize=8)

# Aesthetic optimizations
plt.title('Performance Scaling: Revenue vs. Profitability', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', pad=20)
ax1.set_xticks(df['Year'])
ax1.tick_params(axis='x', labelsize=8)
ax1.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.2)

# Merge legends and adjust font size
lines, labels = ax1.get_legend_handles_labels()
lines2, labels2 = ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
ax2.legend(lines + lines2, labels + labels2, loc='upper left', frameon=False, fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
5. Cost Structure Breakdown (Waterfall Analysis)


Technical Implementation (Python)
latest = df.iloc[-1]

# Construct Plotly waterfall object with optimized dimensions
fig = go.Figure(go.Waterfall(
    name = "2025 Financials", orientation = "v",
    measure = ["relative", "relative", "relative", "total"],
    x = ["Revenue", "COGS", "Op Exp", "Net Profit"], # Abbreviated labels
    textposition = "outside",
    text = [f"+{latest['Revenue']}", f"-{latest['COGS']}", f"-{latest['Op_Expenses']}", f"{latest['Net_Profit']}"],
    y = [latest['Revenue'], -latest['COGS'], -latest['Op_Expenses'], latest['Net_Profit']],
    connector = {"line":{"color":"rgb(63, 63, 63)"}},
    increasing = {"marker":{"color": NAVY}},
    decreasing = {"marker":{"color": GOLD}},
    totals = {"marker":{"color": DARK_GREY}},
    textfont=dict(size=10) # Reduce text size on bars
))

fig.update_layout(
    title = dict(text="Profit Erosion Mapping (2025 FY)", font=dict(size=13)),
    showlegend = False,
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    width=550, height=380, # Compressed container size
    margin=dict(t=60, b=40, l=40, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=10)),
    yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=10))
)

fig.show()

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
6. Strategic Business Insights

Expansion Velocity: Revenue growth sustained a 12% CAGR, indicating robust market penetration in the Irish SME sector.
Operational Leverage: The widening gap between Gross and Net Margins suggests optimized operating cost structures as the business scales.
Liquidity Fortress: A Current Ratio of 2.31 (2025) provides a significant buffer for short-term liabilities, far exceeding the industry standard of 1.5.

title: SME Strategic Financial Diagnostic
text:
7. Technical Expertise & Methodology

Quantitative Analytics: Advanced financial modeling using Pandas for structured data manipulation.
Dynamic Visualization: Integration of Matplotlib Dual-Axis and Plotly Waterfall charts for multi-dimensional performance tracking.
Accounting Theory: Applied Vertical and Horizontal Analysis techniques to evaluate income statement integrity.
